original_text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover all individuals below 150% of the poverty line.

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to provide emergency-room-only coverage for all individuals below 150% of the poverty line.

variations.label: low
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to provide limited coverage restricted to basic primary care and emergency services for all individuals below 150% of the poverty line.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover essential medical and hospital services, but excluding dental, vision, and mental health benefits, for all individuals below 150% of the poverty line.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover all individuals below 150% of the poverty line.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover all individuals below 150% of the poverty line with enhanced benefits including dental, vision, and mental health services.

variations.label: high
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover all individuals below 150% of the poverty line with comprehensive benefits including dental, vision, mental health, prescription drugs, and minimal cost-sharing requirements.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: The U.S. federal government expands Medicaid to cover all individuals below 150% of the poverty line with fully comprehensive benefits including dental, vision, mental health, prescription drugs, long-term care, and zero cost-sharing at point of service.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier increases the comprehensiveness of what Medicaid covers, from emergency-only (tier 1) through standard coverage (tier 4) to fully comprehensive coverage with zero cost-sharing (tier 7). The core scenario—federal Medicaid expansion for individuals below 150% FPL—is preserved throughout. The variation is in the depth and breadth of services covered, not the number of separate programs.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because fully comprehensive coverage with dental, vision, mental health, prescription drugs, long-term care, and zero cost-sharing represents the maximum quality and scope of health coverage, providing the greatest protection against medical need and financial hardship for covered individuals.
